title: agathis palmerstonii
intro: Agathis palmerstonii, also known as Palmer's kauri or Norfolk Island kauri, is a species of coniferous tree native to the Norfolk Island, which is part of Australia. The bark and resin of this tree have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, particularly for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The resin of Agathis palmerstonii is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. Traditionally, the resin is used to prepare herbal remedies, such as ointments and tinctures, which are applied topically to treat skin conditions and respiratory issues.
